english: frq.; ri-taṅ-mtsams-su (ri thang mtshams su) at the foot of the mountains or hills Med.; rir-gáṅ-pa (rir sgang pa) one living in close vicinity to a mountain, W.; gáṅs-ri (gangs ri) an ice-mountain, snowy mountain, glacier, nágs-ri (nags ri) or śiṅ-ri (shing ri) a hill covered with wood, brág-ri (brag ri) a rocky mountain, gyá-ri (gya ri) a mountain or hill consisting of slate-stone or schist; spaṅ-ri (spang ri) a hill covered with grass. — 2. brim of a hat or cap; side-leather, side-piece of a shoe. — 3. symb. num.: 7. — 4. num. figure: 55. — 5. v. ri-mo (ri mo). Comp. and deriv. ri-skéd (ri sked) v. rkéd-pa (rked pa). — ri-skyégs (ri skyegs) Stg., v. skyegs (skyegs). — ri-kród (ri khrod) chain of mountains, assemblage of hills or mountains, esp. as abode of hermits who, on that account, are called ri-kród-pa (ri khrod pa); also directly = dgón-pa (dgon pa) hermitage. — ri-mgó (ri mgo) mountain top. — ri-rgyál (ri rgyal), rii rgyál-po (ri'i rgyal po) a very high mountain, e.g. Tise Mil., Gandharā Sbh., esp. = ri-ráb (ri rab), q.v. — ri-rgyúd (ri rgyud) chain of mountains, ridge of hills. — rí-čan (ri can) mountainous, hilly. — ri-čén (ri chen), ri-bo-čé (ri bo che) a great mountain. — ri-nyín (ri nyin) the sunny side, the southern slope of a mountain. — ri-rnyíl (ri rnyil) fall of a mountain, land-slip Sch. — ri-stóṅ (ri stong) v. stóṅ-pa (stong pa). — ri-deu (ri de'u) (or rdeu (rde'u)) čuṅ (chung) Sch., *ri-bóg* (*ri bog*), *ri-de-bóg* (*ri de bog*) W., a mountain spur abounding in stones. — *ri-dód* (*ri dod*) W. (perh. to be spelled ri-kród (ri khrod)) a hermit (living) in the mountains. — ri-sná (ri sna) mountain spur. — rí-pa (ri pa) an inhabitant of the mountains, mountaineer, from a Tibetan point of view equivalent to the Latin paganus and agrestis as opp. to urbanus, therefore = peasant, poor uncivilized person. — ri-práṅ (ri phrang) a little hill or mountain. — rí-bo (ri bo) = ri (ri), v. above. — ri-bór-pa (ri bor pa) Tar., Cs.: ri-'ór-pa (ri 'or pa); = ri-kród-pa (ri khrod pa), ri-bór-gyi groṅ (ri bor gyi grong) mountain village Tar. — ri-brág (ri brag), brág-ri (brag ri) rocky mountain. — ri-bóg (ri bog) spur. — ri-sbúg (ri sbug) mountain cavern. — ri-rtsá (ri rtsa) foot, ri-rtsé (ri rtse) top of a mountain, nyí-ma ri-rtsé-la póg-na (nyi ma ri rtse la phog na) when the rising sun illumines the mountain tops. — ri-rtsé-kan (ri rtse kan) Cs. n. of a mischievous spirit. — ri-rdzóṅ (ri rdzong) mountain fortress, fort. — ri-ráb (ri rab) the centre of the world and king of the mountains, the fabulous Sumeru or Meru, also ri-rab-lhún-po (ri rab lhun po), ri-rgyál (ri rgyal), ri-bo-mčog-ráb (ri bo mchog rab) Mil. — ri-lúṅ (ri lung) mountain and valley. — ri-gséb (ri gseb) Sch. = ri-kród (ri khrod). — ri-sríbs (ri sribs) the side not exposed to the sun, shady side, north-side of the mountains.
ri-gu (ri gu) young goat, kid W.
ri-rgyal (ri rgyal) Sch.: foxes or fox-skins(?).
ri-dwags (ri dwags) animals of chase, game.

ri-ba (ri ba) W. *rí-če* (*ri che*) to be worth, gen. as adj. worth, *lug di ṅul čig rí-če yin* (*lug di ngul cig ri che yin*) this sheep is worth one rupee W.; dṅul brgya ri-bai rta (dngul brgya ri ba'i rta) a horse worth one hundred rupees Cs.. cf. rin (rin) and rib (rib); ri-bai rin-táṅ (ri ba'i rin thang) the full price Sch.

ri-bóṅ (ri bong) hare, ri-bóṅ-mo (ri bong mo) Cs. female hare; it lives in Ld., but not in the smaller valleys, e.g. not in Lahoul; ri-bóṅ-gi rwa (ri bong gi rwa) the horn of a hare, a nonentity, a thing not existing, cf. mo-šám-gyi bu (mo sham gyi bu).

1. figure, picture, painting, drawing, lha-káṅ-gi (lha khang gi) Glr.; ri-mo-mkán (ri mo mkhan) painter; ri-mo-čan (ri mo can), ri-mo-ldan (ri mo ldan) marked with figures; ri-mor byéd-pa (ri mor byed pa) to represent by means of figures and colours, to paint Do.; markings (streaks, speckles etc.) šai (sha'i) markings of a (tiger's) skin Tar.; ri-krá (ri khra) having stripes of various colours, spotted, speckled; ri-mo (ri mo) also draught, plan, design, and fig. pattern, rule of conduct, law written into the heart. — 2. = rim-gro (rim gro) reverence, veneration, ri-mor byéd-pa (ri mor byed pa) to honour, to venerate Stg.
ri-lu (ri lu) col., but also Tar. 63, for ril-bu (ril bu).
ri-śi (ri shi), ॠषि, = draṅ-sróṅ (drang srong) q. v.
ri-śo (ri sho) n. of a medicinal herb Med.
rig (rig) in Ld. col. and provinc. for žig (zhig): *maṅ-na rig* (*mang na rig*) or *nyuṅ-ṅu rig toṅ* (*nyung ngu rig tong*) give much! give little!

ríg-pa (rig pa) I. vb., 1 to know, to understand, = śés-pa (shes pa) with the termin. of a sbst: to know (a person etc.) as, with the termin. of the inf.: to know that, to perceive, observe, krós-par rig-nas (khros par rig nas) perceiving that he became angry Dzl.; pá-la rig-par gyis (pha la rig par gyis) let your father know it, inform your father of it Tar.; zlóg-tu rig-par byed (zlog tu rig par byed) (it
